mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, depressing equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity and business investment, which mechanically reduces forward equity cash flows and drives negative market returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 55% of historical instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating inflation, a contraction in real savings deposits signals reduced household liquidity and consumption capacity, which compresses corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in > 50% of observations, or the cross-sectional correlation between RSAFS declines and SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth accelerates corporate revenue expectations, which directly lifts equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit a positive mean difference between GDP growth acceleration and deceleration periods (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 98.0, it indicates a supply-side bottleneck reversal or demand collapse that triggers profit margin compression, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly lower during INDPRO breaches of the 98.0 threshold compared to non-breaching periods (p > 0.05), or the signal's hit rate drops below 45% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rises in unemployment and declines in real savings deposits reflect a dual shock to household balance sheets and labor income, which mechanically reduces aggregate demand and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 55% of historical instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening monetary cycle, a rapid rise in the federal funds rate combined with a falling employment count signals aggressive policy restraint that crushes equity valuations and drives negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The mechanism produces positive forward returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for term spread proxies, or the out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.10 over a 2-year window.
